curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.overallDescription: 5 units, 6 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand the need for measurement and distinguish between fundamental and derived units within SI system.
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify physical equations and derive relationships.
Describe motion using elementary concepts of differentiation and integration.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity in non-uniform motion.
Analyze uniformly accelerated motion using relations derived graphically or via calculus.
Perform addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, and dot/cross products on vectors.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components in a plane.
Analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion using vector principles.
Apply Newton's three laws to explain force, inertia, impulse, and momentum changes.
Utilize the law of conservation of linear momentum in collision scenarios.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces and distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction.

units.description: Motion in a Straight Line, Motion in a Plane
Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body

suspicious_chapter_names: Practical Examination for Visually Impaired Students Class XI

competency_groups.title: Physical World and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to units of measurement, SI systems, significant figures, dimensional analysis, and uncertainty determination.
competencies: Understand the need for measurement and distinguish between fundamental and derived units within SI system.
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify physical equations and derive relationships.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Straight Line Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving frame of reference, differentiation/integration for motion description, uniform/non-uniform motion, and graphical analysis.
competencies: Describe motion using elementary concepts of differentiation and integration.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity in non-uniform motion.
Analyze uniformly accelerated motion using relations derived graphically or via calculus.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering vector operations, resolution of vectors, and motion under uniform velocity/acceleration including projectile and circular motion.
competencies: Perform addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, and dot/cross products on vectors.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components in a plane.
Analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion using vector principles.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies regarding Newton's laws, momentum conservation, friction types, and dynamics of circular motion.
competencies: Apply Newton's three laws to explain force, inertia, impulse, and momentum changes.
Utilize the law of conservation of linear momentum in collision scenarios.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces and distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction.

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving work done by constant/variable forces, energy theorems, potential energy concepts, and collision types.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces and relate it to kinetic energy via the work-energy theorem.
Differentiate between conservative and non-conservative forces and analyze motion in vertical circles.
Distinguish between elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering center of mass concepts, torque, angular momentum conservation, moment of inertia, and rotational dynamics.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for particle systems and rigid bodies like uniform rods.
Apply laws of conservation of linear and angular momentum to physical scenarios.
Compare linear and rotational motions using torque, moment of inertia, and equations of rotational motion.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit V: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving Kepler's laws, universal gravitation, gravitational potential/energy, orbital mechanics, and variation in gravity.
competencies: Apply Kepler's laws of planetary motion to describe celestial movements.
Calculate acceleration due to gravity variations with altitude and depth using the universal law of gravitation.
Determine escape speed and orbital velocity for satellites based on energy considerations.

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Solids
competency_groups.description: Competencies regarding elasticity, stress-strain relationships, material moduli, and elastic energy.
competencies: Interpret Hooke's law and define Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus, and Poisson's ratio.
Analyze the relationship between stress and strain in solids under various loads.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Fluids
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering fluid pressure, Pascal's law, viscosity, Bernoulli's theorem, and surface tension phenomena.
competencies: Apply Pascal's law to hydraulic systems and analyze fluid pressure due to gravity.
Explain viscous flow concepts including Stokes' law, terminal velocity, and Reynolds number (critical velocity).
Use Bernoulli's theorem for applications like Torricelli's law and dynamic lift.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Thermal Properties of Matter
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving heat transfer modes, thermal expansion, specific heats, latent heat, and blackbody radiation laws.
competencies: Distinguish between conduction, convection, and radiation in heat transfer processes.
Analyze anomalous expansion of water and calculate calorimetric quantities (Cp, Cv).
Apply Wien's displacement law and Stefan's law to blackbody radiation problems.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering laws of thermodynamics, state variables, and processes like isothermal/adiabatic changes.
competencies: Apply the Zeroth law to define thermal equilibrium and temperature measurement.
Utilize the First Law (conservation of energy) for heat, work, and internal energy calculations.
Analyze reversible, irreversible, isothermal, adiabatic, and cyclic processes using thermodynamic state variables.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the equation of state, kinetic assumptions, molecular speed concepts, and degrees of freedom.
competencies: Derive pressure from kinetic theory assumptions and relate it to temperature via rms speed.
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities of gases.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit IX: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations
competency_groups.description: Competencies regarding periodic motion, simple harmonic motion (SHM), energy in oscillation, and pendulum dynamics.
competencies: Identify conditions for Simple Harmonic Motion and derive equations of motion from uniform circular motion concepts.
Analyze the restoring force and time period of a loaded spring and a simple pendulum.

competency_groups.title: Waves
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering wave types, superposition principle, standing waves, resonance modes, and beats.
competencies: Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and describe displacement relations for progressive waves.

internal_assessment: Not specified in the provided text.

practical_assessment.component: Two experiments one from each section
practical_assessment.details: 7 marks for Section A experiment + 7 marks for Section B experiment
Record of at least 8 Experiments [with 4 from each section] and Record of at least 6 Activities [with 3 each from section A and section B]

project_assessment.details: Report of the project carried out by the students

project_assessment.component: Viva on experiments, activities and project
project_assessment.details: Oral examination covering all practical components

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements
outcomes: Understand the need for measurement and distinguish between fundamental and derived units within SI system.
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify physical equations and derive relationships.
Describe motion using elementary concepts of differentiation and integration.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity in non-uniform motion.
Analyze uniformly accelerated motion using relations derived graphically or via calculus.
Perform addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, and dot/cross products on vectors.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components in a plane.
Analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion using vector principles.
Apply Newton's three laws to explain force, inertia, impulse, and momentum changes.
Utilize the law of conservation of linear momentum in collision scenarios.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces and distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power
outcomes: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces and relate it to kinetic energy via the work-energy theorem.
Differentiate between conservative and non-conservative forces and analyze motion in vertical circles.
Distinguish between elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions.

lesson_planning_intelligence.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Vernier Callipers, Screw Gauge, Spherometer activities and experiments required.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Significant figures rules, dimensional analysis problems.
Graphical analysis of motion, calculus-based relations.
Projectile motion calculations, circular motion dynamics.
Newton's laws application, conservation of momentum problems.

lesson_planning_intelligence.unit_name: Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power
Unit V: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
lesson_planning_intelligence.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Torque and angular momentum conservation problems.
